message:
feat(triggers): implement UPDATE OF column-change filtering with per-row OLD/NEW pseudo-table values

Implement correct UPDATE OF trigger semantics so triggers with explicit
column lists (e.g. UPDATE OF name) only fire when at least one listed
column actually changed between OLD and NEW row snapshots:

- Add trigger_update_of_columns_changed() helper that compares OLD/NEW
  values for each column in the trigger's UPDATE OF list using cmp_values
- Apply the filter in both fire_before_triggers() and fire_after_triggers()
  before evaluating WHEN clauses
- Add 6 comprehensive tests validating trigger row image propagation:
  - INSERT triggers correctly receive NEW row values
  - UPDATE triggers receive both OLD and NEW row values
  - DELETE triggers receive OLD row values
  - WHEN clauses using OLD/NEW comparisons in update context
  - UPDATE OF triggers skip when listed column is unchanged
  - UPDATE OF triggers fire when listed column changes

Completes milestone 2 of bd-1fbkm (P0: OLD/NEW pseudo-table frame stack).
Beads metadata updated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1ene","title":"5D.2: Wire ROLLBACK to use pager transaction rollback and reload state","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:49:52.952323329Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T07:50:10.642751885Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T07:50:10.642723231Z","close_reason":"Implemented reload_memdb_from_pager() method and updated execute_rollback() to use pager-backed state reload instead of MemDatabase snapshot restore. All 19 rollback tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ene","depends_on_id":"bd-35my","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ene","depends_on_id":"bd-ujkm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1680,"issue_id":"bd-1ene","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# 5D.2: Wire ROLLBACK to use pager transaction rollback and reload state

## What

ROLLBACK must discard all changes made during the transaction by rolling back the pager
transaction (which discards dirty pages) and reloading MemDatabase from the rolled-back state.

## Where to change

**Primary:** `crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs` — `execute_rollback()`

## Current code

```rust
fn execute_rollback(&self) -> Result<()> {
    if let Some(mut txn) = self.active_txn.borrow_mut().take() {
        txn.rollback(&Cx::background())?;  // Pager rollback (mostly no-op)
    }

    // Restore MemDatabase from snapshot taken at BEGIN
    if let Some(snapshot) = self.txn_snapshot.borrow_mut().take() {
        *self.db.borrow_mut() = snapshot.db;
        *self.schema.borrow_mut() = snapshot.schema;
    }

    self.db.borrow_mut().rollback_undo();
    *self.in_transaction.borrow_mut() = false;
    self.savepoints.borrow_mut().clear();
    Ok(())
}
```

## Target code

```rust
fn execute_rollback(&self) -> Result<()> {
    let cx = Cx::background();

    if let Some(mut txn) = self.active_txn.borrow_mut().take() {
        // Pager rollback: discard all dirty pages, release writer lock
        txn.rollback(&cx)?;
    }

    // Reload MemDatabase shadow from pager's committed state
    // (The pager now reflects the pre-transaction state)
    self.reload_memdb_from_pager(&cx)?;

    *self.in_transaction.borrow_mut() = false;
    self.savepoints.borrow_mut().clear();
    Ok(())
}

fn reload_memdb_from_pager(&self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<()> {
    // Open read transaction to see committed state
    let txn = self.pager.begin(cx, TransactionMode::Read)?;

    // Clear current MemDatabase
    let mut db = self.db.borrow_mut();
    *db = MemDatabase::new();

    // Reload schema from sqlite_master (reuse 5A.4 logic)
    let schema = self.load_schema_from_pager(cx, &*txn)?;
    *self.schema.borrow_mut() = schema;

    // Reload table data into MemDatabase shadow
    for table_schema in self.schema.borrow().iter() {
        let root_page = PageNumber::new(table_schema.root_page as u32).unwrap();
        self.load_table_into_memdb(cx, &*txn, &mut db, root_page, table_schema)?;
    }

    drop(txn);  // release read transaction
    Ok(())
}
```

## Why reload instead of snapshot restore?

Currently, ROLLBACK restores a MemDatabase snapshot taken at BEGIN time. This works but is
wasteful (deep-cloning the entire MemDatabase at BEGIN is O(data_size)).

With pager-backed persistence, ROLLBACK just tells the pager to discard dirty pages. The
pager's committed state IS the pre-transaction state. We reload from there.

Eventually (after MemDatabase removal), ROLLBACK becomes trivial: just tell the pager to
rollback. No MemDatabase reload needed because reads go through the pager directly.

## Tests

- `test_rollback_discards_inserts`: BEGIN, INSERT, ROLLBACK → data not visible
- `test_rollback_restores_state`: INSERT+COMMIT, BEGIN, INSERT more, ROLLBACK → only first INSERT visible
- `test_rollback_outside_txn_is_noop`: ROLLBACK without BEGIN → error or no-op (SQLite returns error)

 {"id":"bd-1eog","title":"Bench: RaptorQ encode/decode microbench matrix (K,T,loss)","description":"Create a microbenchmark matrix for RaptorQ encode/decode costs.

## Matrix Axes
- `K_source`: small (1-32), medium (256-1024), large (4096+)
- `T`: MTU-ish (<=1366), page-ish (4096)
- loss rate: 0%, 5%, 10%, 20%

## What To Measure
- systematic fast path throughput (MB/s) and CPU
- repair symbol generation throughput
- decode throughput and p95/p99 latency to completion
- hash/auth verification cost

## Acceptance Criteria
- Benchmarks are deterministic and can run in “smoke” mode in CI.
- Results are written down as defaults justification for `T` and overhead%.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:58:17.684512922Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T00:38:20.991374281Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T00:38:20.991347811Z","close_reason":"Benchmark matrix complete: bench file covers K axis (1-4096), T axis (1366/4096), loss rates (0-20%), with smoke mode for CI. Compliance test bd_1eog_raptorq_microbench_matrix_compliance.rs passes all 10 tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1eog","depends_on_id":"bd-1l30","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1eog","depends_on_id":"bd-26ge","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1eos","title":"§5.9.0 Coordinator IPC Transport (Cross-Process, Unix Domain Socket)","description":"STATUS: CLOSED (superseded rollup)

This bead is a consolidated/rolled-up version of §5.9.0 created during an earlier spec-to-beads conversion pass.

DO NOT implement from this rollup directly.

Use these replacement beads instead (plan-of-record):
- bd-1m07 — §5.9.0 Coordinator IPC Transport: Unix Socket Protocol + Wire Schemas
- bd-3ipx — §5.9.0.1 Wire Payload Schemas: Tagged Unions + Canonical Ordering + Size Caps

---

SECTION: §5.9.0 (spec lines ~9168-9430)

PURPOSE: Implement the cross-process coordinator IPC transport using Unix domain sockets.

## Write Coordinator Overview (§5.9)
- Single background task serializing commit sequencing critical section
- Compatibility mode (WAL): serializes validation, WAL append, fsync/group-commit, version publishing
- Native mode (ECS): tiny-marker sequencer -- never moves page payload bytes
  - Writers persist CommitCapsule objects concurrently
  - Coordinator validates, allocates commit_seq, persists CommitProof, appends tiny CommitMarker
- Multi-process: coordinator is a ROLE (not thread in every process)
  - Exactly one process holds coordinator role (lease-backed)
  - Other processes route commit publication through coordinator

## §5.9.0 Coordinator IPC Transport (normative, Unix)

### Socket Endpoint
- Per-database Unix socket path:
  - Native mode: foo.db.fsqlite/coordinator.sock
  - WAL mode: foo.db.fsqlite/coordinator-wal.sock
- Socket directory: 0700 permissions
- Socket file: 0600 permissions

### Peer Authentication (REQUIRED)
- On accept: MUST call UnixStream::peer_cred()
- MUST reject any peer whose uid doesn't match database owner's UID
- Optional: connection-level MAC cookie from DatabaseId + per-install secret

### Framing (normative, length-delimited)
- Frame { len_be: u32, version_be: u16 (=1), kind_be: u16, request_id: u64_be, payload: [u8] }
- All header integers big-endian (network byte order)
- len_be >= 12 (header-only) and <= 4 MiB; reject outside range
- Payload encoding: canonical + deterministic; integers little-endian unless specified
- Canonical ordering: sets sorted with no duplicates (ObjectId arrays lexicographic, pages ascending)

### Reserve/Submit Discipline (normative, two-phase)
1. RESERVE: client requests commit pipeline slot → permit_id or BUSY
2. SUBMIT_*: client submits exactly one request bound to permit_id
- Dropping connection without submit MUST free permit
- Bound on outstanding permits: default 16 (same derivation as §4.5)
- permit_id is connection-scoped, single-use capability

### Idempotency (REQUIRED)
- Every SUBMIT carries TxnToken
- Coordinator treats (txn_id, txn_epoch) as idempotency key
- If terminal decision already produced → return same response to duplicate SUBMIT

### Bulk Payload Transfer (REQUIRED)
- MUST NOT send full page bytes inline in frames
- WAL commits: large write sets transferred via spill file descriptor (SCM_RIGHTS)
- Uses asupersync::net::unix::{UnixStream, SocketAncillary, AncillaryMessage}

### Wire Message Kinds (V1, kind_be values)
1: RESERVE, 2: SUBMIT_NATIVE_PUBLISH, 3: SUBMIT_WAL_COMMIT
4: ROWID_RESERVE, 5: RESPONSE, 6: PING, 7: PONG
Unknown kinds MUST be rejected

### Wire Payload Schemas (normative, V1)
- Common atoms: ObjectId (16 bytes), TxnToken (txn_id:u64, txn_epoch:u32, pad:u32)
- Tagged union encoding: outer tag is ONLY discriminant, no nested tag

#### RESERVE payload: purpose:u8, pad, txn:TxnToken
#### RESERVE response: tag(Ok/Busy/Err), body(permit_id | retry_after_ms | code)

#### SUBMIT_NATIVE_PUBLISH payload:
  permit_id, txn, begin_seq, capsule_object_id, capsule_digest_32
  write_set_summary (canonical u32_le array, sorted ascending, no dupes)
  read/write/edge/merge witness arrays (ObjectId, sorted lexicographic)
  abort_policy:u8

#### SUBMIT_WAL_COMMIT payload:
  permit_id, txn, mode:u8, snapshot_high, schema_epoch
  has_in_rw, has_out_rw, wal_fec_r
  spill_pages: [SpillPageV1 { pgno, offset, len, xxh3_64 }]
  MUST carry exactly one fd via SCM_RIGHTS

#### Response payloads: NativePublishRespV1 (Ok/Conflict/Aborted/Err), WalCommitRespV1 (Ok/Conflict/IoError/Err)

#### ROWID_RESERVE: txn, schema_epoch, table_id, count
  Response: Ok { start_rowid, count } | Err { code }

### Wire Size Caps
- write_set_summary_len <= 1 MiB, must be multiple of 4
- Total witness/edge array counts <= 65,536 per commit
- Any frame > 4 MiB MUST be rejected

### Internal Architecture
- Per-connection handler task translates wire frames to internal requests
- Awaits internal oneshot response, writes RESPONSE frame

DEPENDS ON: bd-3t3.5 (SharedMemoryLayout), bd-3t3.1 (Core Types)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:44:18.749014168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T17:39:20.355084584Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T06:20:09.379293959Z","close_reason":"Content merged into bd-1m07","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1eos","depends_on_id":"bd-3t3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1eos","depends_on_id":"bd-3t3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1eos","depends_on_id":"bd-3t3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1fbkm","title":"P0: OLD/NEW pseudo-table frame stack for trigger execution","description":"## Problem

CREATE TRIGGER execution is partially implemented — trigger bodies execute via execute_statement(), but **OLD/NEW pseudo-table access in trigger body statements and WHEN clauses is not functional**. Line 2721 in crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs has a TODO: "proper OLD/NEW access via frame stack".

Currently:
- fire_before_triggers() and fire_after_triggers() accept _old_values and _new_values but they are always None
- WHEN clauses with OLD/NEW references silently evaluate to true (always fire)
- Trigger body statements cannot reference OLD.column or NEW.column

## Context: NOT a blocker for MCP Agent Mail

MCP Agent Mail's only triggers are FTS5 index maintenance triggers. These are being decommissioned via Search V3 migration (br-2tnl.8.4). Once FTS5 is removed, no triggers are needed, so this bead does NOT block the am DbConn migration to FrankenConnection.

This bead IS critical for **frankensqlite's own maturity** as a general-purpose SQLite replacement. Any application using triggers (which is extremely common in SQLite) needs this.

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Define TriggerFrame context structure
```rust
struct TriggerFrame {
    old_row: Option<Vec<SqliteValue>>,  // None for INSERT triggers
    new_row: Option<Vec<SqliteValue>>,  // None for DELETE triggers
    table_name: String,
    column_names: Vec<String>,          // Column name → index mapping
}
```

### 2. Add frame stack to connection state
```rust
// In Connection struct:
trigger_frame_stack: Vec<TriggerFrame>,
```
Stack supports nested triggers (trigger body DML fires another trigger → push new frame).

### 3. Push frame in fire_*_triggers()
In fire_before_triggers() (line 2688) and fire_after_triggers() (line 2730):
- Construct TriggerFrame from DML old/new row values
- Push onto trigger_frame_stack before executing trigger body
- Pop after trigger body completes (even on error — use RAII guard)

### 4. Resolve OLD.col / NEW.col in expression evaluator
When evaluating column references in trigger body context:
1. Check if trigger_frame_stack is non-empty
2. For "OLD.column": look up column index in frame.column_names, return frame.old_row[idx]
3. For "NEW.column": look up column index in frame.column_names, return frame.new_row[idx]
4. For unqualified "column" in trigger context: check current table first, then OLD/NEW

### 5. Wire WHEN clause evaluation
The trigger_when_matches() helper (lines 711-721) currently falls back to true on evaluation failure. Wire OLD/NEW frame into WHEN clause evaluation so it can reference NEW.status = 'active' etc.

### 6. Handle UPDATE OF column-list filtering
UPDATE triggers with `OF col1, col2` should only fire when specified columns actually change. Compare OLD.col vs NEW.col for the listed columns.

### 7. Implement RAISE() function
RAISE(IGNORE), RAISE(ABORT, msg), RAISE(ROLLBACK, msg), RAISE(FAIL, msg) in trigger bodies.

## Key Files
- crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs — lines 2637-2765 (trigger execution)
- crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs — expression evaluator (column resolution)
- crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs — trigger_when_matches() helper

## Test Plan

### Unit Tests (25+ tests in trigger_tests.rs)

**Basic OLD/NEW access (8 tests):**
1. AFTER INSERT trigger reads NEW.col1, NEW.col2, NEW.col3
2. AFTER DELETE trigger reads OLD.col1, OLD.col2
3. AFTER UPDATE trigger reads both OLD.col and NEW.col
4. BEFORE INSERT trigger reads NEW values
5. BEFORE UPDATE trigger reads OLD and NEW values
6. BEFORE DELETE trigger reads OLD values
7. NEW is None in DELETE trigger context (error or null)
8. OLD is None in INSERT trigger context (error or null)

**WHEN clause (5 tests):**
9. WHEN NEW.status = 'active' — fires only when condition met
10. WHEN OLD.count > 10 — fires only when condition met
11. WHEN NEW.value != OLD.value — fires on actual change
12. WHEN with complex expression (AND/OR/NOT)
13. WHEN false — trigger never fires

**Column indexing and edge cases (5 tests):**
14. Trigger on table with 20+ columns — column index correctness
15. Column names case-insensitive (NEW.Name vs NEW.name)
16. Aliased table names in trigger body
17. Subquery in trigger body referencing NEW values
18. NULL column values in OLD/NEW

**Nested triggers (3 tests):**
19. Trigger A fires DML that fires Trigger B — both have correct OLD/NEW
20. Three-level nesting — frame stack correctness
21. Nested trigger error — outer trigger rolls back cleanly

**UPDATE OF (2 tests):**
22. UPDATE OF col1 — trigger fires when col1 changes
23. UPDATE OF col1 — trigger does NOT fire when only col2 changes

**RAISE function (4 tests):**
24. RAISE(ABORT, 'msg') — aborts statement, keeps prior changes
25. RAISE(ROLLBACK, 'msg') — rolls back entire transaction
26. RAISE(FAIL, 'msg') — fails current row but keeps prior
27. RAISE(IGNORE) — skips current row silently

**FTS5-style triggers (3 tests):**
28. AFTER INSERT trigger: INSERT INTO log_table VALUES(NEW.id, NEW.name)
29. AFTER DELETE trigger: DELETE FROM log_table WHERE id = OLD.id
30. AFTER UPDATE trigger: UPDATE log_table SET name = NEW.name WHERE id = OLD.id

### E2E Test Script: tests/e2e/test_trigger_old_new.sh
```
Tests: 35 assertions, 12 cases
Logging: JSONL structured output with per-assertion timing, input, expected, actual
Cases:
  1. Basic INSERT trigger with NEW access
  2. Basic DELETE trigger with OLD access
  3. Basic UPDATE trigger with OLD+NEW access
  4. WHEN clause filtering
  5. Multi-column trigger correctness
  6. Nested trigger (2 levels)
  7. UPDATE OF column filtering
  8. RAISE(ABORT) behavior
  9. RAISE(ROLLBACK) behavior
  10. RAISE(IGNORE) behavior
  11. Large table (100 columns) trigger performance
  12. Concurrent triggers under BEGIN CONCURRENT
```

Each case logs structured JSONL:
```json
{"case": 1, "assertion": "INSERT trigger NEW.name", "status": "PASS", "expected": "alice", "actual": "alice", "elapsed_ms": 2}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 30 unit tests pass
- E2E test script passes with 35 assertions
- No regression in 5 existing trigger tests
- Frame stack properly cleaned up on error (no resource leaks)
- Zero unsafe code
- Nested triggers work to at least 3 levels deep","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"PlumRabbit","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:00:12.782376336Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T07:12:28.532142149Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3020,"issue_id":"bd-1fbkm","author":"PlumRabbit","text":"Claimed after bv triage as highest-impact open P0 candidate with unclaimed ownership. Starting implementation in connection trigger pipeline (OLD/NEW frame stack + evaluation path) with file reservations active via Agent Mail. First milestone: wire frame context into trigger execution and WHEN evaluation; second milestone: add targeted tests and run rch validation.","created_at":"2026-02-16T07:00:16Z"},{"id":3021,"issue_id":"bd-1fbkm","author":"PlumRabbit","text":"Implemented first execution slice for OLD/NEW trigger frame support in crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs:\n- Added TriggerFrame model + connection-local trigger_frame_stack with push/pop guard.\n- Wired fire_before_triggers/fire_after_triggers to construct/push frame and evaluate WHEN via frame-aware binding.\n- Added AST rewrite pass that binds OLD/NEW pseudo-columns to literal values in trigger body statements before recursive execute_statement.\n- Added focused tests: test_trigger_when_clause_uses_new_frame_values and test_trigger_body_binds_old_new_columns_from_frame.\n\nValidation:\n- PASS: rch exec -- cargo fmt --check\n- PASS: rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-core test_trigger_ -- --nocapture (4 tests, including new ones)\n- PASS: rch exec -- cargo check -p fsqlite-core --all-targets\n\nKnown pre-existing blockers:\n- clippy across fsqlite-core targets fails on existing unrelated lint debt in por.rs and repair_engine.rs.\n- Some rch workers (vmi1149989) intermittently fail with missing /tmp/rch/frankensqlite/asupersync/Cargo.toml.","created_at":"2026-02-16T07:12:28Z"}]}

### E2E Test Script: tests/e2e/test_trigger_old_new.sh
```
Tests: 35 assertions, 12 cases
Logging: JSONL structured output with per-assertion timing, input, expected, actual
Cases:
  1. Basic INSERT trigger with NEW access

  8. RAISE(ABORT) behavior
  9. RAISE(ROLLBACK) behavior
  10. RAISE(IGNORE) behavior
  11. Large table (100 columns) trigger performance
  12. Concurrent triggers under BEGIN CONCURRENT
```

Each case logs structured JSONL:
```json
{"case": 1, "assertion": "INSERT trigger NEW.name", "status": "PASS", "expected": "alice", "actual": "alice", "elapsed_ms": 2}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 30 unit tests pass
- E2E test script passes with 35 assertions
- No regression in 5 existing trigger tests
- Frame stack properly cleaned up on error (no resource leaks)
- Zero unsafe code
- Nested triggers work to at least 3 levels deep","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"PlumRabbit","created_at":"2026-02-15T04:00:12.782376336Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T14:09:02.943291801Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3020,"issue_id":"bd-1fbkm","author":"PlumRabbit","text":"Claimed after bv triage as highest-impact open P0 candidate with unclaimed ownership.  {"id":"bd-1fez","title":"Benchmark: mixed OLTP workload (80% read / 20% write)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:19:20.664829218Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T06:44:38.516362958Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T06:44:38.516336799Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fez","depends_on_id":"bd-1pdl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1681,"issue_id":"bd-1fez","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation matches the task title: Benchmark: mixed OLTP workload (80% read / 20% write) 
- All unit tests pass; code compiles without warnings under clippy pedantic+nursery
- Integration with existing e2e harness framework verified
- No unsafe code, no panics in normal operation
","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:20:09Z"},{"id":1682,"issue_id":"bd-1fez","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Task: Benchmark — mixed OLTP workload (80% read / 20% write)

### What
Simulate a realistic OLTP workload: mostly reads with occasional writes. This is the most realistic benchmark for typical application use.

### Workload Profile
- 80% SELECT (point lookups, range scans, aggregates)
- 15% INSERT (new rows)
- 3% UPDATE (modify existing rows)
- 2% DELETE (remove rows)

### Concurrent Configuration
- 4 reader threads + 1 writer thread
- Reader threads execute SELECT queries continuously
- Writer thread executes INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE operations
- Both run for fixed duration (30 seconds)

### Metrics
- **Read throughput**: queries/sec across all reader threads
- **Write throughput**: mutations/sec for the writer thread
- **Read latency under write load**: Does the writer degrade reader performance?
- **Write latency under read load**: Do readers slow down the writer?

### Why This Matters
Real applications rarely have pure write or pure read workloads. This benchmark shows the practical impact of MVCC:
- C SQLite: Writer holds WAL_WRITE_LOCK, readers can proceed (WAL mode) but writer blocks other writers
- FrankenSQLite: MVCC means readers and writers never interfere

### Acceptance Criteria
- Mixed workload benchmark runs for configurable duration
- Reports both read and write throughput
- Shows latency impact of concurrent access
- Compares FrankenSQLite vs C SQLite head-to-head
","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:20:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1fpm","title":"Establish 'Great Detailed Logging' Standard for Test Suites","description":"## SUMMARY
Define and enforce the high-fidelity logging standard required for all unit and E2E tests. "Vibes-based" debugging is forbidden; tests must produce artifacts that allow deterministic reproduction.

## REQUIREMENTS
1. **Structured Events:** All tests MUST emit structured logs (JSON/tracing) for key lifecycle events: Setup, Step, Assertion, Teardown.
2. **Repro Bundles:** E2E failures MUST emit a directory containing: final DB snapshot, WAL file, RNG seed, and full trace log.
3. **Determinism:** Log content (excluding timestamps in metadata) MUST be deterministic for a given seed. No pointer addresses or memory layout artifacts in logs.
4. **Oracle Diffing:** Conformance tests MUST log the exact diff between FrankenSQLite and C SQLite outputs on mismatch.

## IMPLEMENTATION
- Create `fsqlite-harness::log` module with standardized subscribers.
- Add a CI check that validates the required artifacts/logs exist for at least one smoke test and that `events.jsonl` is valid JSONL.

## Unit Test Requirements

- `test_log_bundle_meta_json_schema_valid`: `meta.json` contains required fields and parses.
- `test_events_jsonl_is_valid_jsonl`: `events.jsonl` is valid JSONL and each event matches the minimal schema.
- `test_bundle_contains_required_files`: missing required artifacts causes a clear, actionable failure.

## E2E Test

- `test_e2e_harness_emits_repro_bundle_on_failure`:
  - Run a known-failing harness case.
  - Assert a deterministic artifact bundle directory is emitted containing `meta.json`, `events.jsonl`, and any engine artifacts (db/wal/shm when applicable).


## Logging Requirements

- INFO: bundle root path + `{suite, case_id, seed}`.
- WARN: large bundles (size threshold) with a summary of included artifacts.
- ERROR: missing/invalid artifact files with file path and parse error.


## Acceptance Criteria
- A single, documented test logging standard exists (JSON event schema + artifact bundle layout) and is implemented in `fsqlite-harness::log`.
- Unit tests and E2E runners can opt into the standard via a single initializer (no per-test boilerplate beyond providing `suite/case_id/seed`).
- On failure, the harness emits a deterministic artifact bundle directory containing at minimum:
  - `meta.json` (suite, case_id, seed, versions)
  - `events.jsonl` (structured logs)
  - `stdout.log` / `stderr.log`
  - DB snapshot + WAL/SHM (when applicable)
  - oracle outputs + diff (for conformance tests)
- Any conformance mismatch logs a machine-readable diff payload including `{ case_id, sql, params, oracle_result, franken_result, diff }`.","acceptance_criteria":"AC:
- A single, documented test logging standard exists (JSON event schema + artifact bundle layout) and is implemented in `fsqlite-harness::log`.
- Unit tests and E2E runners can opt into the standard via a single initializer (no per-test boilerplate beyond providing `suite/case_id/seed`).
- On failure, the harness emits a deterministic artifact bundle directory containing at minimum:
  - `meta.json` (suite, case_id, seed, versions)
  - `events.jsonl` (structured logs)
  - `stdout.log` / `stderr.log`
  - DB snapshot + WAL/SHM (when applicable)
  - oracle outputs + diff (for conformance tests)
- At least one CI smoke test validates `events.jsonl` is valid JSONL and contains `run_start`/`run_end` plus per-step events.
- Any conformance mismatch logs a machine-readable diff payload including `{ case_id, sql, params, oracle_result, franken_result, diff }`.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T10:49:43.351698190Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:21:40.340202126Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:21:40.340178131Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["logging","quality"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fpm","depends_on_id":"bd-21c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1683,"issue_id":"bd-1fpm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented bd-1fpm harness logging standard.

Delivered:
- Added `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/log.rs` with deterministic repro-bundle API:
  - `init_repro_bundle()` initializer (suite/case_id/seed)
  - required artifacts: `meta.json`, `events.jsonl`, `stdout.log`, `stderr.log`
  - structured lifecycle events (`run_start`/`run_end`, setup/step/assertion/teardown)
  - `record_conformance_diff()` machine-readable oracle diff payload
  - validators: `validate_required_files`, `validate_bundle_meta`, `validate_events_jsonl`, `validate_bundle`
- Exported module via `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/lib.rs`.
- Added deps in `crates/fsqlite-harness/Cargo.toml`: `serde`, `serde_json`, `tracing`.
- Added tests in `crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/logging_standard.rs`:
  - `test_log_bundle_meta_json_schema_valid`
  - `test_events_jsonl_is_valid_jsonl`
  - `test_bundle_contains_required_files`
  - `test_e2e_harness_emits_repro_bundle_on_failure`

Validation run:
- `cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --test logging_standard -- --nocapture` ✅ (4 passed)
- `cargo check --all-targets` ✅
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` ❌ blocked by unrelated reserved file `crates/fsqlite-core/src/lib.rs` (`clippy::assertions_on_constants`), owner notified via Agent Mail.
- `cargo fmt --check` ❌ blocked by unrelated pre-existing formatting diffs in other crates (`fsqlite-mvcc`, `fsqlite-pager`).
- `ubs --only=rust,toml crates/fsqlite-harness` ✅ (exit 0)

Also applied a minimal compile-unblock fix in `crates/fsqlite-vfs/src/unix.rs` (borrow-check E0502 refactor + dead_code annotation on existing helper) to allow workspace checks to progress.

 {"id":"bd-1ft5","title":"§17.6 Fuzz Test Specifications: SQL Parser + Record Format + Wire Protocol Fuzzing","description":"E2E Test:
- test_e2e_bd_1ft5_compliance: concrete E2E identifier for structure lint compliance.

## SUMMARY
Defines fuzz test specifications for SQL parser, record format, B-tree page decoder, WAL frame decoder, JSON parser, and RaptorQ decoder. Uses both raw byte fuzzing (libfuzzer/cargo-fuzz) and grammar-based structured fuzzing via the `arbitrary` crate for deeper coverage. The SQL parser fuzz target accepts arbitrary bytes and must never panic or loop forever. The grammar-based fuzzer generates structured SQL from FuzzStatement variants, executes it, and verifies no panics or corruption (with PRAGMA integrity_check on success). All fuzz targets follow the "must not panic, must not corrupt" discipline.

## KEY DATA STRUCTURES / ALGORITHMS
- **SQL Parser Raw Fuzz Target:** `fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]| { ... })` — converts bytes to UTF-8, calls `fsqlite_parser::parse(sql)`, must not panic or loop.
- **Grammar-Based SQL Fuzzer:** `#[derive(Arbitrary)] enum FuzzStatement { Select(FuzzSelect), Insert(FuzzInsert), ... }` with `to_sql()` method. Execute against database, verify no panic/corruption, run PRAGMA integrity_check on Ok results.
- **Record Decoder Fuzz Target:** Arbitrary bytes -> `decode_record()` -> must not panic.
- **B-tree Page Decoder Fuzz Target:** Arbitrary 4096-byte pages -> page parser -> must not panic.
- **WAL Frame Decoder Fuzz Target:** Arbitrary frame bytes -> frame parser -> must not panic.
- **JSON Parser Fuzz Target:** Arbitrary bytes -> `json_valid()` -> returns 0 or 1, must not panic.
- **RaptorQ Decoder Fuzz Target:** Valid encoding with random bit flips -> decoder either succeeds with correct output or returns error, NEVER silent corruption.
- **Fuzzing Infrastructure:** cargo-fuzz with libfuzzer backend, `arbitrary` crate for structured input generation.

## NORMATIVE INVARIANTS
- INV-FUZZ-1: SQL parser MUST NOT panic on any input bytes (valid UTF-8 or not).
- INV-FUZZ-2: SQL parser MUST NOT loop forever on any input.
- INV-FUZZ-3: Grammar-based SQL execution MUST NOT corrupt the database. PRAGMA integrity_check MUST pass after every successful execution.
- INV-FUZZ-4: Record decoder MUST NOT panic on arbitrary byte inputs.
- INV-FUZZ-5: B-tree page decoder MUST NOT panic on arbitrary 4096-byte inputs.
- INV-FUZZ-6: WAL frame decoder MUST NOT panic on arbitrary byte inputs.
- INV-FUZZ-7: JSON parser MUST return 0 or 1 for any input, never panic.
- INV-FUZZ-8: RaptorQ decoder MUST either succeed with correct output or return an error — NEVER silent corruption.

## UNIT TEST REQUIREMENTS
- `fuzz_sql_parser_raw_bytes`: Fuzz target accepting &[u8], converting to UTF-8, parsing. Must not panic.
- `fuzz_sql_parser_grammar_based`: Fuzz target using FuzzStatement::Arbitrary -> to_sql() -> execute. Verify no panic, no corruption, integrity_check passes.
- `fuzz_record_decoder`: Fuzz target accepting arbitrary bytes -> decode_record(). Must not panic.
- `fuzz_btree_page_decoder`: Fuzz target accepting arbitrary 4096-byte pages -> page parser. Must not panic.
- `fuzz_wal_frame_decoder`: Fuzz target accepting arbitrary frame bytes -> frame parser. Must not panic.
- `fuzz_json_parser`: Fuzz target accepting arbitrary bytes -> json_valid(). Must return 0 or 1, no panic.
- `fuzz_raptorq_decoder`: Fuzz target with valid encoding + random bit flips -> decoder. Either correct output or error, never silent corruption.
- `test_fuzz_sql_parser_known_crashes`: Regression tests for any previously discovered crash inputs (corpus seeds).
- `test_fuzz_grammar_integrity_check_after_execution`: Execute 100 random FuzzStatements, run PRAGMA integrity_check after each — all must pass.

- test_e2e_bd_1ft5_compliance: concrete E2E identifier for structure lint compliance.
Run each fuzz target for a minimum of 1 million iterations (or 1 hour wall time, whichever comes first) on CI. Verify zero panics, zero hangs (timeout per iteration), zero silent corruptions. Maintain a corpus of interesting inputs per fuzz target. Run regression tests for all previously discovered crash inputs. For the grammar-based SQL fuzzer, verify PRAGMA integrity_check passes after every batch of 1000 executions.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- All 7 fuzz targets (SQL raw, SQL grammar, record, B-tree page, WAL frame, JSON, RaptorQ) implemented and integrated with cargo-fuzz.
- Grammar-based SQL fuzzer uses #[derive(Arbitrary)] FuzzStatement with to_sql() method.
- Zero panics across all fuzz targets after sustained fuzzing.
- PRAGMA integrity_check passes after grammar-based SQL fuzzing sessions.
- RaptorQ decoder never produces silent corruption (either correct output or explicit error).
- Crash inputs are added to regression corpus and run as #[test] cases.
- Fuzz targets run in CI with minimum iteration/time budget.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:04:52.164393844Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T09:52:07.654566133Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T09:52:07.654526038Z","close_reason":"Compliance gate identifiers and logging checks shipped in harness test slice","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ft5","depends_on_id":"bd-18zh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ft5","depends_on_id":"bd-1a32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ft5","depends_on_id":"bd-1fpm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ft5","depends_on_id":"bd-21c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1684,"issue_id":"bd-1ft5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §17.6 Fuzz Tests (from P2 bd-2de5)

**SQL parser fuzz:** Arbitrary bytes -> parse() must not panic or loop.
**Grammar-based SQL fuzzing:** `arbitrary` crate for structured SQL. Execute, verify no panic/corruption, PRAGMA integrity_check if Ok.
**Other targets:** record_decoder, btree_page_decoder, wal_frame_decoder, json_parser, raptorq_decoder (correct output or error, never silent corruption).
","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:23:06Z"},{"id":1685,"issue_id":"bd-1ft5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §17.6 Fuzz Test Specifications

### Spec Content (Lines 16637-16678)

**SQL parser fuzz target:**
Located at fuzz/fuzz_targets/sql_parser.rs. Takes arbitrary &[u8], converts to UTF-8, passes to fsqlite_parser::parse(). Must not panic, must not loop forever.

**Grammar-based SQL fuzzing:**
Uses `arbitrary` crate to generate structured SQL from the grammar (not just random bytes) for deeper coverage. FuzzStatement enum derives Arbitrary with variants FuzzSelect, FuzzInsert, etc. Each variant has to_sql() method. Fuzz target converts to SQL, executes against database. Must not panic, must not corrupt database. If Ok, verify with PRAGMA integrity_check.

**Other fuzz targets:**
- record_decoder: arbitrary bytes -> decode_record() -> must not panic
- btree_page_decoder: arbitrary 4096-byte pages -> page parser -> no panic
- wal_frame_decoder: arbitrary frame bytes -> frame parser -> no panic
- json_parser: arbitrary bytes -> json_valid() returns 0 or 1, no panic
- raptorq_decoder: valid encoding with random bit flips -> decoder either succeeds with correct output or returns error, never silent corruption

### Unit Tests Required
1. test_fuzz_sql_parser_utf8: Parse arbitrary valid UTF-8 strings, must not panic
2. test_fuzz_sql_parser_non_utf8: Non-UTF-8 bytes gracefully rejected (no panic)
3. test_fuzz_sql_parser_no_infinite_loop: Parser terminates for all inputs within timeout
4. test_fuzz_grammar_select: Grammar-based FuzzSelect generates valid SQL, execution does not corrupt database
5. test_fuzz_grammar_insert: Grammar-based FuzzInsert generates valid SQL, integrity_check passes after execution
6. test_fuzz_grammar_integrity_check: After any successful grammar-based fuzz execution, PRAGMA integrity_check returns "ok"
7. test_fuzz_record_decoder_no_panic: Arbitrary bytes passed to decode_record() must not panic
8. test_fuzz_btree_page_decoder_no_panic: Arbitrary 4096-byte pages passed to page parser must not panic
9. test_fuzz_wal_frame_decoder_no_panic: Arbitrary frame bytes passed to frame parser must not panic
10. test_fuzz_json_parser_no_panic: Arbitrary bytes passed to json_valid() returns 0 or 1, must not panic
11. test_fuzz_raptorq_decoder_no_corruption: Valid encoding with random bit flips either succeeds with correct output or returns error, never silent corruption
12. test_fuzz_raptorq_decoder_bitflip_detection: Random bit flips in encoded data are detected (not silently accepted)

### E2E Test
End-to-end validation: Run all fuzz targets for a minimum duration (e.g., 60 seconds each in CI, longer in nightly). SQL parser fuzz target processes thousands of random byte sequences without panic or hang. Grammar-based fuzzer generates hundreds of structured SQL statements (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE), executes each against an in-memory database, runs PRAGMA integrity_check after each successful execution, and verifies no corruption. Record decoder, B-tree page decoder, WAL frame decoder, and JSON parser fuzz targets all process arbitrary inputs without panicking. RaptorQ decoder receives valid encodings with injected bit flips and either reconstructs correctly or returns an explicit error -- never produces silently corrupted output. Fuzz corpus is retained for regression testing.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:30:28Z"},{"id":1686,"issue_id":"bd-1ft5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Logging Requirements

- INFO: fuzz run summary: `target`, `cases`, `crashes`, `duration_ms`.
- ERROR: on crash, emit seed + minimized input artifact path.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T07:42:46Z"},{"id":1687,"issue_id":"bd-1ft5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## E2E Test (Named)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit test identifiers for this bead are concrete and executable.
- [ ] E2E identifier for this bead is concrete and executable.
- [ ] Logging requirements include DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR and bd-1fpm alignment.","created_at":"2026-02-09T03:10:10Z"},{"id":1689,"issue_id":"bd-1ft5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented compliance gate slice: crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/bd_1ft5_fuzz_specifications_compliance.rs. Enforces DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR + fuzz summary fields + bd-1fpm reference. Validation: cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --test bd_1ft5_fuzz_specifications_compliance (pass), ubs --only=rust on file (pass), cargo check -p fsqlite-harness --test bd_1ft5_fuzz_specifications_compliance (pass with existing harness warnings).","created_at":"2026-02-09T09:52:07Z"}]}
